content: Long-term memory compression and summarization techniques designed for human and AI retention can be repurposed to build curious, open-ended autonomous agents that gradually distill actionable, generalizable prior experience across millions of small interaction trials instead of storing raw interaction data. This creates agents that retain core strategic knowledge for long-horizon tasks while avoiding the bloat of storing every low-level motion and interaction outcome.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
